macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 and Chip Stocks Slide as AI Rally Faces Sustainability Questions
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures slid Friday as a deepening selloff in chip stocks sent the S&P 500 down 1.01%, sparking concerns about the sustainability of the AI-driven surge amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions.

items.title: Fed Policymakers Debate Rate Hikes as Inflation Concerns Persist
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus of policymakers arguing interest rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a potential dissent at Chairman Kevin Warsh's upcoming July meeting.

items.title: Netflix Earnings Miss and Chip Selloff Drag Markets Lower
items.summary: Netflix tumbled after its earnings report added to market pressure, while major chip stocks faced their steepest weekly decline in over a year, pushing major averages toward a losing week.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to One-Month High Amid US-Iran Strait of Hormuz Crisis
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel and WTI rose 3.50% as escalating hostilities between the U.S. and Iran threaten the Strait of Hormuz, with the IEA chief warning of critical global energy security risks.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, reversing their previous ranking in the global tech race.

items.title: US to Impose 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Over Unfair Trade Practices
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 25% tariffs on select Brazilian imports including beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials following a yearlong investigation into alleged unfair trade practices.

items.title: Big Banks Report Booming Q2 Revenue Amid SpaceX IPO and Lending Rebound
items.summary: Major U.S. banks are set to report strong second-quarter revenue driven by the SpaceX IPO, a rebound in commercial lending, and resilience despite Iran war volatility.

items.title: China Holds Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged Despite Slower Q2 Growth
items.summary: China is expected to keep benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month in July, even as softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data highlights uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $63K Amid U.S.-Iran Conflict and AI Selloff
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped over 2% to around $63,200 as escalating Middle East tensions and a global AI stock selloff triggered broad crypto market declines, with Ethereum falling 3.5% to $1,820 and Solana losing nearly 3% to $74[6].

items.title: European Authorities Seize $1.51B Cryptomixer Bitcoin-Mixing Service
items.summary: Europol dismantled the Cryptomixer platform used by ransomware groups to launder bitcoin, seizing servers, data, and $29 million in BTC as part of a $1.51 billion operation targeting crypto-mixing services[1].

items.title: FTX Distributes $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Payout Wave
items.summary: FTX is distributing roughly $900 million to creditors in its fifth wave of payouts from the collapsed exchange, marking a significant step in the bankruptcy resolution process[2].

items.title: T. Rowe Price Launches First Active Multi-Token Crypto ETF TKNZ on NYSE
items.summary: Asset manager T. Rowe Price launched TKNZ, the first active multi-token crypto ETF on the NYSE, exposing investors to Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and other cryptocurrencies with $1.89 trillion under management[3][5].

items.title: Polkadot Plunges 11% Breaking Below $2.05 Support Level
items.summary: Polkadot (DOT) collapsed 11% to $2.02 amid a broader crypto selloff, breaking below the psychological $2.00 support level on massive trading volume[1].

items.title: US Senate Discusses Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Act to Purchase 1 Million BTC
items.summary: The U.S. Senate is debating the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Act, which intends to purchase 1 million BTC as part of a national strategic reserve initiative[4].

items.title: Morgan Stanley Unlocks Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana Trading on E*TRADE
items.summary: Morgan Stanley has enabled trading of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana on the E*TRADE platform, expanding institutional access to major cryptocurrencies[7].

items.title: Nigerian President Establishes Virtual Asset Regulatory Framework
items.summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu signed an executive order creating a regulatory framework for virtual assets and establishing a virtual asset commission, with the CBN launching a regulatory sandbox[4].

items.title: Crypto.com Raises $400 Million from Citadel Securities at $20B Valuation
items.summary: CEX Crypto.com raised $400 million from Citadel Securities at a $20 billion valuation, signaling continued institutional fundraising momentum despite cautious market sentiment[3].

items.title: $431 Million in Crypto Liquidations as Market Cap Drops 2.1%
items.summary: Aggregate cryptocurrency market capitalization declined 2.1% to $2.17 trillion with $431 million in liquidations over 24 hours amid AI selloff and Middle East tensions[10].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with a 1-million-token context window and native vision, claiming it is the world's first open 3T-class frontier model that rivals Anthropic's Fable.

items.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Releases Open-Weight 'Inkling' Model
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, led by former OpenAI executive Mira Murati, released Inkling, a 975B-parameter open-weight Mixture-of-Experts foundation model with 41B active parameters and native reasoning across text, images, audio, and video.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red to Curb Prompt-Injection Attacks on GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming LLM that reduced prompt-injection attack success rates on GPT-5.6 from over 90% to 23%, significantly improving security against adversarial inputs.

items.title: Meta and Anthropic in Talks for $10B Data Center Capacity Deal
items.summary: The New York Times reports Meta is negotiating to rent computing capacity from its data centers to Anthropic in a potential deal worth roughly $10 billion over two years to support AI model training.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billions in AI Data Center Capacity
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Anthropic Restores Access to Fable and Mythos Models After Export Control Review
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its frontier Fable and Mythos models following a federal export control review that had previously restricted them.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech rankings as investors reassess the artificial intelligence outlook and Apple's AI system secured government approval in China.

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188B Following $3B Investment Led by Coatue Management
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion, according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: EU Issues New Rules Requiring Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rival AI Companies
items.summary: The European Union has issued new regulations requiring Google to share search data and open its Android system to rival AI companies to foster competition in the sector.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Groundbreaking Analog AI Chip for Deep Neural Networks
items.summary: IBM Research announced a new analog AI chip demonstrating remarkable efficiency and accuracy in performing complex computations for deep neural networks, potentially advancing hardware capabilities for AI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced sells over 3 million copies in first week
items.summary: Ubisoft's remake of Assassin's Creed 4: Black Flag has surpassed 3 million copies sold globally just one week after its July 9 launch, with over 100,000 peak concurrent players on Steam alone.

items.title: Bethesda confirms Obsidian Entertainment is returning to Fallout with new game
items.summary: Obsidian, the studio behind Fallout: New Vegas, has been officially confirmed by Bethesda to be developing a new post-apocalyptic game in the Fallout universe, marking their return to the franchise.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Mobile global release canceled, China servers closing September 2026
items.summary: Square Enix and Tencent have canceled the planned global release of Final Fantasy XIV Mobile, with the Chinese version set to shut down on September 30, 2026, after just over a year of service.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 'Pacific Front' launches July 21 with Top Gun: Maverick collaboration
items.summary: EA and Battlefield Studios announced that Battlefield 6's fourth season will arrive July 21 on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, featuring a collaboration with Top Gun: Maverick starting August 18.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1 update celebrates second anniversary with new agents and modes
items.summary: HoYoverse revealed that Zenless Zone Zero's Version 3.1 update will launch July 29 across all platforms, featuring new agents, a permanent mode, events, and rewards for the game's second anniversary.

items.title: Denuvo removed from Assassin's Creed Shadows, game now launches without workarounds
items.summary: Fans' long-standing demand has been met as Denuvo has been removed from Assassin's Creed Shadows, allowing the game to launch directly without requiring any bypass methods.

items.title: Gallipoli tactical WWI shooter gets August 20 release date
items.summary: Blackmill Games announced that Gallipoli, its 25v25 tactical World War I multiplayer shooter, will launch August 20 on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store with a 25% launch discount.

items.title: Xbox workers organize against layoffs as 1,600 jobs still at risk
items.summary: Xbox employees are fighting back against ongoing layoffs, with approximately 1,600 jobs still potentially affected as the company continues its restructuring efforts.

items.title: Mortal Shell 2 sequel receives release date announcement
items.summary: The sequel to the well-received Souls-like game Mortal Shell has officially received a release date announcement, continuing the franchise's journey.

items.title: Dent Attack becomes week's most acclaimed release with 88% OpenCritic score
items.summary: Dent Attack, a unique game where players control a train instead of a person in a Tony Hawk-style gameplay, has received an 88% score on OpenCritic with 97% positive Steam reviews.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Unseating Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, marking a reshuffle in tech heavyweights as investors reassess the outlook for artificial intelligence investments.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees, alleging they misappropriated trade secrets to develop the iPhone maker's own yet-to-be-unveiled AI hardware.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide Billions in Data Center Capacity to U.S. Defense
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Meta in Talks to Rent $10B Computing Power to Anthropic
items.summary: Meta is reportedly negotiating to rent computing power from its data centers to Anthropic in a deal worth approximately $10 billion over two years, highlighting the scarcity of AI computing resources.

items.title: Judge Rejects Meta Employees' Bid to Block Layoffs Amid AI Discrimination Claims
items.summary: A U.S. judge rejected a request by 26 Meta employees to block layoffs while they pursue claims that AI-powered tools targeted them for job cuts due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department Allows Federal Employees to Download TikTok
items.summary: The U.S. Justice Department announced on Friday that federal employees are now permitted to download the short-video app TikTok on government devices.

items.title: Asus Re-enters India's Android Tablet Market with Pad T3201
items.summary: Asus is set to launch the Pad T3201 tablet in India on August 6, featuring a 12.2-inch OLED display and MediaTek Dimensity 8300 processor.

items.title: Databricks Hits $188B Valuation
items.summary: Databricks has reached a valuation of $188 billion, extending its position as a leading second-tier player in the AI infrastructure space.

items.title: San Francisco Orders Apple and Google to Remove AI Undressing Apps
items.summary: San Francisco has issued an order requiring Apple and Google to remove 'nudify' apps from their platforms due to safety and privacy concerns.

items.title: European Union Issues New Rules Requiring Google to Share Search Data
items.summary: The European Union has mandated that Google share search data and open its Android system to rival AI companies under new regulatory rules.
